# Phase 6 Requirements Quality Checklist: Fluxos de Eco de Nota

**Purpose**: Validar se os requisitos da Phase 6 sao completos, claros, rastreaveis e fechaveis antes de tratar a feature `002-note-echo-flows` como encerrada.
**Created**: 2026-05-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Scope And Authority

- [X] CHK001 Are Phase 6 requirements explicit that this phase is documentation, traceability and gate closure only, with no new product capability? [Clarity, Blueprint §Phase 6]
- [X] CHK002 Are the boundaries between the closed `001-auth-day-surface` baseline and active `002-note-echo-flows` traceability requirements stated without ambiguity? [Consistency, Tasks §T046, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK003 Does the Phase 6 requirement set state that inherited baseline tests may remain but cannot prove this feature without the `002-note-echo-flows:` prefix? [Clarity, Tasks §T046]
- [X] CHK004 Are mentions inline `@nota` still specified as out of scope during Phase 6 closure, so canon updates cannot accidentally promote them as delivered? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK005 Does Phase 6 define which artifact has authority if root-canon instructions name files that are currently represented under `docs-canonical/*` instead of root-level paths? [Conflict, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK006 Are `docs/` and `docs-canonical/*` roles distinguished clearly enough that neither can silently override the current canon authority? [Clarity, Constitution §I, Blueprint §T047]

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK007 Are all Phase 6 tasks T046 through T055 mapped to an explicit closure outcome, not merely an activity label? [Completeness, Tasks §Phase 6]
- [X] CHK008 Are the required inputs for T047 named fully enough to prevent canon edits based only on memory or historical docs? [Completeness, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK009 Are all feature behaviors that need canon absorption enumerated: direct echoes, Reader relations, cross-day navigation, manual add/remove echo, candidate pagination, atomic continuation and task/note separation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001..FR-024, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK010 Are the requirements for `CHANGELOG.md` complete enough to avoid duplicating Phase 5 fix history while still consolidating the full feature outcome? [Clarity, Blueprint §T048]
- [X] CHK011 Are requirements defined for reviewing `DRIFT-LOG.md` when no drift entry is needed, including what evidence must be recorded for "reviewed without patch"? [Completeness, Blueprint §T049]
- [X] CHK012 Are requirements defined for reviewing `CANON-MIGRATION-COVERAGE.md` only when historical material is absorbed or reinterpreted? [Completeness, Blueprint §T049]
- [X] CHK013 Does Phase 6 specify whether current partial changelog entries for US2/US3 are sufficient, must be consolidated, or must be superseded by a final feature entry? [Ambiguity, Blueprint §T048]
- [X] CHK014 Are closure requirements clear about whether `README.md`, `CURRENT-STATE.md` and `ROADMAP.md` must represent the whole feature as delivered, not only a subset of stories? [Completeness, Blueprint §T047]

## Traceability Quality

- [X] CHK015 Is the `@req` tag format fully specified, including feature id, requirement id family, numbering range and one-tag-per-line expectation? [Clarity, Tasks §T046, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK016 Are all expected suites for FR-001 through FR-024 and SC-001 through SC-006 listed with enough specificity to avoid relying on broad search output alone? [Completeness, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK017 Does the traceability map cover both user-facing stories and technical safety contracts such as RPC atomicity, rollback and absence of task fields? [Coverage, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-018, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK018 Are requirements explicit for how to handle legacy unprefixed `@req FR-*` / `@req SC-*` tags in files touched by this feature? [Clarity, Tasks §T046]
- [X] CHK019 Are requirements explicit for how to handle unprefixed tags in baseline-only suites so Phase 6 does not create unnecessary churn? [Boundary, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK020 Is FR-024/SC-006 coverage required inside a feature-scoped suite if the only current proof lives in a baseline regression suite? [Completeness, Blueprint §T046]
- [X] CHK021 Can every Phase 6 traceability requirement be objectively measured by the named `rg` commands plus human review criteria? [Measurability, Blueprint §T046]

## Canon Update Quality

- [X] CHK022 Does T047 specify the expected semantic update per canon file rather than only naming files to edit? [Completeness, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK023 Are data-model requirements for `note_echoes`, `manual_link`, `continue_note`, `context_day`, `notes.day` and absence of task fields defined with enough precision for canon review? [Clarity, Blueprint §T047, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-018]
- [X] CHK024 Are architecture requirements for `useDayEntries`, direct-count derivation, contextual Reader, cross-day navigation and one-shot `pendingReaderOpen` described as product architecture rather than implementation trivia? [Clarity, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK025 Are requirements for `REQUIREMENTS.md` scoped to delivered behaviors while preserving `@nota` inline as excluded? [Consistency, Spec §FR-024, Blueprint §T047]
- [ ] CHK026 Are `TEST-SPEC` requirements explicit about both feature-scoped suites and final gates, including `doc:score` as reported maturity rather than merge-blocking proof by itself? [Clarity, Blueprint §T047, Blueprint §T054]
- [ ] CHK027 Does Phase 6 specify how root canon updates should handle already-present partial canon statements, so the final patch consolidates rather than contradicts them? [Ambiguity, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK028 Are requirements clear that `docs-canonical/*` contains the executable canons, root docs contain governance/status, and `docs/` remains historical support without becoming current authority? [Consistency, Constitution §I, Blueprint §T047]

## Security And Data Boundaries

- [X] CHK029 Are security requirements for `public.continue_note` complete: `SECURITY DEFINER`, `auth.uid()` checks, fixed `search_path`, minimal authenticated grant, unauthenticated/cross-user failure and no partial write? [Completeness, Blueprint §T047, Spec §FR-016]
- [X] CHK030 Are requirements explicit that `service_role` must remain absent from the client and must not be introduced as a Phase 6 shortcut? [Consistency, Constitution §V, Plan §Restrictions, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK031 Are RLS/RPC canon requirements tied to the exact user-visible guarantee that failed continuation cannot leave an orphan note? [Traceability, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-005, Blueprint §T047]
- [X] CHK032 Are requirements clear that note continuation uses `notes.day` and `context_day` without importing task vocabulary such as `source_day`, `target_day` or `scheduled_at`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Blueprint §T047]
- [ ] CHK033 Are rollback and forward-migration requirements for the RPC sufficiently documented as closure evidence rather than implied by test existence? [Gap, Blueprint §T055]

## Gate And Evidence Quality

- [X] CHK034 Are the closure gates ordered with explicit dependencies so document gates run after canon/changelog/drift review, and technical gates run after implementation-scope edits? [Completeness, Blueprint §T050..T054]
- [X] CHK035 Are exit-code expectations and output preservation requirements specified for lint, test, typecheck, doc:guard and doc:score? [Measurability, Blueprint §T050..T055]
- [X] CHK036 Does the doc:guard requirement define the Windows stray-line exception narrowly enough to avoid masking a failing guard? [Clarity, Blueprint §T053]
- [X] CHK037 Does the doc:score requirement demand reporting the real score without converting a low score into a hidden pass? [Clarity, Blueprint §T054]
- [X] CHK038 Is the final evidence block specified with all required fields: T046 status, canon paths, docs-canonical status, drift/coverage review, command outputs, RPC evidence, DocGuard limitation and Phase 7/baseline non-reopening confirmation? [Completeness, Blueprint §T055]
- [X] CHK039 Are requirements explicit that DocGuard PASS is necessary but not sufficient evidence of semantic canon alignment? [Consistency, Tasks §T055, Blueprint §T055]
- [ ] CHK040 Are requirements clear about where the final evidence block must live if there is no PR body yet? [Gap, Blueprint §T055]

## Scenario And Edge Coverage

- [ ] CHK041 Are exception scenarios for lost auth, unavailable related notes, duplicate/self-links, failed RPC and reload/navigation races all tied to either traceability tags or canon closure requirements? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-020, Spec §FR-021, Spec §SC-005]
- [X] CHK042 Are non-functional requirements for documentation quality, auditability and traceability measurable enough to support closure without relying on reviewer intuition? [Measurability, Tasks §T046..T055]
- [X] CHK043 Are requirements defined for preserving the note/task boundary during Phase 6 documentation edits, including ghost-card exclusivity for tasks? [Consistency, Constitution §III, Spec §FR-018, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK044 Are requirements defined for proving that Phase 6 did not implement Phase 7, did not reopen `001-auth-day-surface`, and did not expand scope into inline mentions? [Boundary, Blueprint §T055, Spec §FR-024]
- [ ] CHK045 Are assumptions about QA-internal success criteria still appropriate for closure, or does Phase 6 need a documented replacement evidence type because the phase is not performing live QA? [Assumption, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK046 Are requirements clear for handling any newly discovered mismatch between implemented behavior, active spec and root canon during Phase 6: patch canon, register drift, or reopen a task? [Gap, Blueprint §T047, Blueprint §T049]

## Notes

- This checklist tests whether Phase 6 requirements are written well enough to close the feature honestly.
- It does not verify whether code, docs, tests or gates already pass.
- Items referencing `[Gap]`, `[Ambiguity]` or `[Conflict]` should be resolved in the Phase 6 requirements or closure evidence before declaring the branch merge-ready.
